name: Niter Kebbeh (Ethiopia)
recipeInstructions:
Niter Kebbeh is a spiced-up ghee. Here's a recipe for it from "The Africa News Cookbook", published in the US by Viking Penguin.
1. Slowly melt butter in a saucepan over low heat.
2. Once melted, bring to a boil and allow the foam to settle on top.
3. Add the chopped onion, garlic, ginger, turmeric, cardamom seeds, cinnamon, cloves, and nutmeg.
4. Simmer uncovered on the lowest heat setting for 45 to 60 minutes until the surface becomes transparent and milk solids accumulate at the bottom of the pan.
5. Carefully pour off the clear liquid into a separate container, discarding the spices and solids.
6. Refrigerate the clarified butter. Straining it through a double layer of damp cheesecloth will help remove any remaining impurities.
7. The prepared Niter Kebbeh will keep in the refrigerator for up to 2 to 3 months when stored properly.
